title: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple Cannot Kill Avatar: Fire & Ash At The MLK Weekend Box Office
text: The highly anticipated horror sequel 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple failed to dethrone Avatar: Fire and Ash at the MLK weekend box office. Despite pre-release projections suggesting a potential $20-22 million four-day opening that could lead the weekend, the film underperformed significantly. Avatar: Fire and Ash secured the number one position for its fifth consecutive weekend with $17.8 million, while The Bone Temple earned $15 million over the four-day period. The 28 Years Later sequel is receiving solid critical praise with a 92% critics' score on Rotten Tomatoes and an A- CinemaScore, suggesting audiences who see the film are enjoying it. However, the reduced opening weekend gross indicates limited mainstream appeal and possibly audience fatigue from the quick sequel. The release of The Bone Temple just seven months after the previous 28 Years Later installment may have affected awareness and ticket sales.
